rulings.Case Title: ERNEST MORGAN VS. MIRIAM Y. DRUHET-JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renotice Of Hearing On Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for AUG 17, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANTs EMILE DRUHET, CAMERON DRUHET, ROGERS DRUHET, DESHONNE DRUHET, SKYLAR DRUHET, JAMES DRUHET, CRAIG DRUHET, DEXTER SELLERS, TANYA HARRISON, and ALLISON BROWN's Hearing On Motion To Set Aside Default Moot in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to Alison Brown, Emile Druhet, and Tanya Harrison CCP 473(b) is not available as the time has run. Also they failed to show excusable neglect, surprise or mistake. Any relief based on Court's equitable powers and extrinsic fraud is not available in this case as no extrinsic fraud has been shown as to Alison Brown, Emile Druhet, and Tanya Harrison. The Court also notes that authority in support of the equitable relief request was cited for the first time in the "Supplemental Points and Authorities" filed after opposition had been filed and 3 months after the moving papers had been filed. Motion is MOOT as to the other moving defendants (as conceded by Plaintiff in opposition). Other defendants appeared by answer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL BERRY ET AL VS. CHIK-KIN CHAU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for AUG 17, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANTs CHIK-KIN CHAU, XIU HE CHAU, and WEN ZHOU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to comply with requirements of Ellis Act and SFRO 37.9A. Specifically, payment has not been made to the tenants at the time of the service of the notice (operative notice upon which this Unlawful Detainer is based). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that payments made in conjunction with previously served notices satisfy the requirement of SFRO 37.9A(e)(3)(A) (payment "shall paid at the time of the service of the notice of termination of tenancy). Additionally, one of the owners of record failed to comply with SFRO 37.9A(f)(1) and certify that "that actions have been initiated as required by law to terminate existing tenancies." Plaintiff cited no authority for the proposition that such failure may be rectified by certification made by a co-owner or by attachment of the Notice of Termination. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. ANN DARSKY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for AUG 17, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT ANN DARSKY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Exemption from SFRO is sufficiently alleged and challenge to the veracity of this allegation is not proper on the demurrer. Attached to the complaint is a 45 page lease and all pages are present. = (501/REQ)
